title: task
description: tun.rs:123-148 - The send() method calls libc::write() once and returns Ok(()) regardless of how many bytes were actually written. If the write returns fewer bytes than requested (common on slow/congested devices), data is silently truncated. Failure scenario: Under load, TUN write returns partial write of N < buf.len() bytes, but send() returns success. The truncated packet is injected into the kernel, causing IPv6 checksum failures or protocol errors. Fix: Loop until all bytes are written or return error.
close_reason: Already fixed: The send() method at lines 123-152 already loops until all bytes are written via 'while written < buf.len()' with 'written += n' on each successful partial write
